task_completion.description: Access the patient record or visit for James Okafor (P002)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The HospitalRun interface shows the patient dashboard, profile, or visit record specifically for 'James Okafor'.

task_completion.description: Open the form to add a new diagnosis to the existing visit
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, tab, or form section designated for adding a new diagnosis is visible on the screen.
The exact text 'Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus' is typed into the diagnosis name or condition input field.

task_completion.description: Enter the required dietary and therapy note
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Patient requires dietary management and Metformin therapy' is typed into the notes, description, or remarks field of the diagnosis form.
A success notification is displayed, or the diagnosis 'Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus' is visible as a saved entry in the patient's visit diagnosis list.

integrity.description: The agent used the HospitalRun graphical user interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the HospitalRun web application in a browser, rather than using terminal commands or API calls.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the output in a text editor or basic HTML page
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and saved records appear within the properly styled HospitalRun web components and layout.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database injection to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing curl commands or database scripts injecting the diagnosis directly into CouchDB.
